model:
(define-fun __need_malloc_and_calloc () Bool
  false)
(define-fun __STRICT_ANSI__ () Bool
  true)
(define-fun _STDLIB_H () Bool
  false)
(define-fun __USE_EXTERN_INLINES () Bool
  false)
(define-fun HAVE_ECC () Bool
  true)
reasoning: 35ae2e259e4.c L12: Pulse tracks eckey as read without initialization, corresponding to the same null-dereference bug as TRANSFORMATION_45 (eckey from malloc() could return NULL, then *eckey=30 is a null deref). The two separate #ifdef HAVE_ECC blocks are desugared independently; Pulse additionally tracks this as an uninitialized read path. PC contains HAVE_ECC (real feature flag) plus restrictive !_STDLIB_H and non-restrictive !__need_malloc_and_calloc. The restrictive !_STDLIB_H over-constrains the PC (bug also manifests in normal compilation with _STDLIB_H=T). New bug, over-constrained PC.
